extracted.title: MNCA Operational Flow Order -- LIFTED EFF 2/3
extracted.description:
Effective 9:00 AM CCT February 3, 2026, Maritimes & Northeast Canada (MNCA) is lifting the Operational Flow Order issued on January 24, 2026.

As a reminder, MNCA requires all shippers and point operators to carefully review demand for gas and schedule gas consistent with daily needs and to tender and receive gas consistent with confirmed nominations regardless of their cumulative imbalance position.

Shippers are required to coordinate any imbalance payback through MNC's account representative and actual payback quantities may be limited depending on system operations and conditions. To the extent shippers and/or point operators do not adhere to this requirement, MNC may issue customer specific OFOs pursuant to General Terms and Conditions Section 10, in order to maintain the operational integrity of the system.

Please contact your Operations Account Representative if you have any questions.

Please refer to the table below for the effected MNC operational areas, dates, and tolerance percentages:

Operational Area: System Wide
Gas Day Feb 1 and Feb 2: OFO In Effect; No Due Pipe OFO 10% / 400
Gas Day Feb 3 and until further notice: No OFO; Payback must be coordinated

NOTE:
Deliveries No DP: Shippers are not to take more than scheduled
Deliveries No DS: Shippers are not to take less than scheduled
Receipts No DP: Shippers are not to give less than scheduled
Receipts No DS: Shippers are not to give more than scheduled
